channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Nine workers killed in armed assault at copper-gold mining site in Chagai, Balochistan
item.description: At least nine people lost their lives in a tragic armed attack at a copper and gold mining project site in Balochistan’s Chagai district, according to local authorities and official confirmations. The...

item.title: Government proposes NDIS changes affecting 160,000 participants
item.description: Health Minister Mark Butler has announced a major overhaul of the National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS) aimed at reining in costs. The changes will tighten eligibility requirements, potentially ...

item.title: Complete Guide to UP Board Student Scholarships 2026
item.description: The article was intended to provide a detailed guide on scholarships available for students of the UP Board in the year 2026. It would typically cover the eligibility criteria, including academic perf...

item.title: Victims' families welcome ICC confirmation of crimes against Duterte over drug war killings
item.description: Families of victims of the Philippine government’s anti-drug campaign under former President Rodrigo Duterte have expressed their support and relief following the International Criminal Court’s (ICC) ...

item.title: Former hospital executive arrested in investigation linked to Lucy Letby case over alleged obstruction of justice
item.description: Cheshire Constabulary has confirmed a new arrest as part of its ongoing investigation into corporate and gross negligence manslaughter at the Countess of Chester Hospital, where nurse Lucy Letby murde...

item.title: IKEA Unveils Innovative Inflatable Easy Chair in PS 2026 Collection
item.description: IKEA has reintroduced inflatable furniture with its new PS 2026 Easy Chair, marking the brand's first significant return to the concept since the a.i.r range of the late 1990s. The chair combines a ca...

item.title: Remembering the Victims of the Lapu Lapu Festival Attack in Vancouver
item.description: One year ago, the Lapu Lapu Day festival in East Vancouver was marred by a horrific attack that claimed 11 lives and injured many others. Adam Kai-Ji Lo has been charged with 11 counts of second-degre...

item.title: U.S. investigation examines deaths and disappearances of scientists, including Canadian astrophysicist, amid conspiracy claims
item.description: A conspiracy theory circulating in the United States suggesting a coordinated link between the deaths and disappearances of scientists with ties to sensitive research has prompted a multi-agency revie...

item.title: Senate inquiry examines Australia’s LNG exports and whether gas taxation delivers fair returns compared to Japan
item.description: Concerns are mounting in Australia over whether the country is receiving adequate financial returns from its liquefied natural gas (LNG) exports, particularly those supplied to Japan. A Senate Select ...

item.title: Malacañang acknowledges ICC confirmation of crimes against humanity charges against former President Duterte
item.description: Malacañang has expressed respect for the decision of the International Criminal Court (ICC) after it confirmed charges of crimes against humanity against former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte in...

item.title: Rain-on-snow flooding exposes risks in aging dam infrastructure in the Great Lakes region
item.description: Michigan and parts of Wisconsin experienced severe flooding in spring 2026 after heavy rainfall fell on top of accumulated snow, overwhelming rivers, lakes, and dams. Nearly half of Michigan’s countie...

item.title: Mamata Banerjee says early polling trends show TMC leading in West Bengal Assembly elections
item.description: West Bengal Chief Minister and Trinamool Congress (TMC) supremo Mamata Banerjee has claimed that the polling trends observed so far in the first phase of the West Bengal Assembly elections indicate a ...

item.title: Beganovic Departs TS Galaxy, Reflects Proudly on Achievements Amid Squad Challenges
item.description: Outgoing TS Galaxy head coach Adnan Beganovic has expressed pride in his three-and-a-half-year tenure at the club, despite leaving before the Nedbank Cup final. Appointed in November 2024 following Se...

item.title: The Origins and Conflict Behind GrapheneOS and CopperheadOS
item.description: Daniel Micay, a Canadian security researcher, co-created CopperheadOS with James Donaldson to enhance Android security. CopperheadOS gained attention for its 'hardening' techniques, which added layers...

item.description: Air Tanzania Company Limited (ATCL) is preparing for the 2027 Africa Cup of Nations (AFCON) by launching exclusive travel packages for both local and international football fans. The event, which Tanz...

item.title: Africa strengthens AI and data protection rules amid rising stablecoin use in finance
item.description: As financial institutions across Africa increasingly integrate stablecoins into core operations such as treasury management and cross-border payments, regulators are stepping up efforts to strengthen ...

item.title: UK and France agree to deploy riot police on beaches to curb Channel migrant crossings
item.description: The UK and France have struck a new deal to combat illegal small boat crossings in the English Channel. Under the agreement, worth £662 million over three years, a 50-strong squad of French riot polic...

item.title: Cursor AI startup linked to Pakistani co-founder enters $60bn acquisition arrangement with SpaceX
item.description: Cursor, an AI code-generation startup co-founded by Pakistani-born entrepreneur Sualeh Asif, has reportedly entered into a major $60 billion acquisition arrangement with Elon Musk’s SpaceX, according ...

item.title: Former and current female premiers assess challenges facing Quebec’s new premier Christine Fréchette
